mission.title: prometheus: Add option to log slow queries and recording rules
mission.description: Add option to log slow queries and recording rules. This issue affects 41+ users.

steps.title: Identify prometheus analyze symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your prometheus deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus -n prometheus --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Add option to log slow queries and recording rules

steps.title: Review prometheus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus
```
Having something like [PostgreSQL's log_min_duration_statement](http://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/static/runtime-config-logging.html) would be useful to debug performance problems.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add option to log slow queries and recording rules
steps.description:
2 change sets:
- Remove the global map of contexts from the common/route library, to match https://github.com/prometheus/common/pull/79
- Add some basic opentracing hooks into the HTTP handlers and query path.  Not a huge amount, just enough to show what you can do.

This has beed mooted as a solution to #1315.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Add option to log slow queries and recording rules is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus -n prometheus --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
2 change sets:
- Remove the global map of contexts from the common/route library, to match https://github.com/prometheus/common/pull/79
- Add some basic opentracing hooks into the HTTP handlers and query path.  Not a huge amount, just enough to show what you can do.

This has beed mooted as a solution to #1315.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
